title: Calliandra rekoi
taxonomy: Calliandra rekoi is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Calliandra, which contains around 200 species of plants. The Calliandra genus is a part of the tribe Phaseoleae, and further classified into the subfamily Faboideae. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a type of shrub or small tree, known for its vibrant flowers and attractive foliage.

The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. intro: Calliandra rekoi, also known as the "Powder Puff Tree," is a species of flowering tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Calliandra rekoi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calliandra rekoi include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and as a natural remedy for colds and flu.
